other_observers: Ken Pals, Cheri Orwig, Dave Wade; also seen by Andy Bankert and photographed at close range by Glenn Walbek
description: First photographed overhead in strong late-afternoon light showing short neck, white secondaries and red bill (see 2 photos from 9/14/2014). Photographed again on 9/15/2014 resting at swim beach. Also flew around briefly, landed back at swim beach, then flew off again. Very short red legs, shorter than other tern species present. Very pale gray mantle and primaries, with gray carpal bar very evident, but not as dark as seen on some of the hatch-year Common Terns. Entirely red bill, slightly shorter than that of Common Terns. Inside of bill very bright red when bill held open. Head rounder than other terns present, with partial blackish cap but not as dark as that of Common Terns; white forehead. Extended wings when backlit, showing bright white, translucent secondaries. (trailing edge of inner wing; see photo from 9/15). In flight, smaller than Forster's Terns nearby, and appeared short-necked. Does not show well in photo, but belly had a faint gray wash, raising questions about this bird's age, i.e. a juvenile or HY versus a subadult, or SY.
photographer: Photographer: N Komar, N Komar, N Komar, N Komar, N Komar
